THE COURT WILL NOT DETERMINE THE ADEQUACY OF THOSE RESPONSES ON THIS MOTION. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE INCOMPLETE OF FEBRUARY 14, 2006 AND THE SUBSEQUENT LATE RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $650, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Admissions, Request For Document Production, Form And Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-27-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Joe Yeary's Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Admissions, Request For Document Production, Form and Special Interrogatories is moot as further responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. The court finds that the incomplete of February 14, 2006 and the subsequent late responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Plaintiff in the amount of $650, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
